name: ignore_operator_in_rule
description: ~ operator on a rule ignores its semantic value from parent

name: ignore_not_predicate_semantic_value
description: Not-predicate semantic value is ignored in AST
grammar:
START <- _ !DUMMY HELLO_WORLD '.'
HELLO_WORLD <- HELLO 'World' _
HELLO <- 'Hello' _
DUMMY <- 'dummy' _
~_ <- [ \t\r\n]*

name: ignore_and_predicate_semantic_value
description: And-predicate semantic value is ignored in AST

name: skip_token_with_tilde
description: Tilde on whitespace rule ignores its semantic value

name: ignore_drops_only_target_in_ast
description: ~ on a reference drops only that node's AST value, keeping siblings

name: ignore_middle_reference_in_ast
description: ~ in the middle of a sequence keeps the non-ignored siblings

name: ignore_repeated_reference_in_ast
description: (~A)+ matches but contributes no AST children
